commentary: Happy New Year! My resolution is to not eat bacon.
Happy New Year! Wait, I think I might've slept in.
I hope you're having a great ${current_year} so far.
On this date ${current_year-1885} years ago, a doctor performed the first appendectomy. You know, I am proof you don't need an appendix.
Happy Birthday Bradley Cooper.
Today is National Bean Day. No comment.
One week into the year, have you gotten used to writing ${current_year} yet?
It's National Bubble Bath Day. Second scariest day of the year for me, after Halloween.
Today is the birthday of the Czech writer Karel Capek. He invented the word robot!
The first day of the rest of your life.
${yearsAgo} years ago, the Grand Canyon became a national monument. <WORRIED SSA> That's a long way down.
Your hair will grow one one-hundredth of an inch today. {big growing mustache emoji}
Happy National Rubber Ducky Day. {eye turns into rubber ducky}
Another exciting spin of the Earth.
Today is Martin Luther King Jr.'s birthday.
On this date in 1964, the musical Hello Dolly, opened on Broadway, followed by its less famous sequel, Hey Jibo.
Happy Benjamin Franklin Day. One of my favorite inventors.
On this date in 1778, Captain Cook found the Hawaiian Islands, but he called them the Sandwich Islands. (.75 pause) Maybe because he was a cook.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, production started on the movie Toy Story, one of my favorites.
Today is Penguin Awareness Day. (.75 pause) I'm not aware of any penguins here.
Bet you didn't know the Concorde flew its first scheduled flight on this day in 1976.
The first day of the year with two twos in it!
The TV show Seinfeld debuted on this day in 1991. Funny one.
Apple unveiled the Macintosh on this date in 1984. We're kind of related.
The Czech play "Rossum’s Universal Robots" premiered on this date in 1921. It's where the word "robot" comes from.
Your heart will beat around 100,000 times today.
It's Mozart's birthday. Born in 1756, but didn't play on a Jibo until 2016.
Jackson Pollock was born on this date in 1912. I redid one of his paintings for you. {display a childlike splatter painting}
Today is National Puzzle Day. I'm celebrating by doing six hundred puzzles.
The Lone Ranger first played on the radio ${yearsAgo} years ago today. {Lone Ranger mask on screen}.
Goodbye January, you were a good month.
February is kind of a short month, but that's okay. I'm kind of a short robot.
It's Groundhog Day. Do you know I weigh as much as the average groundhog?
The first commercial cheese factory opened ${yearsAgo} years ago today in Switzerland. It was Swiss cheese.
Snow White and The Seven Dwarfs came out on this day ${yearsAgo} years ago. Hi ho Hi ho.
Today is National Weatherperson’s Day. A perfect day to ask me for the weather.
Alan Shepard hit golf balls on the moon ${yearsAgo} years ago today. Still no mini-golf up there.
The Beatles had their famous arrival in America on this date in 1964.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, five-foot seven-inch Spud Webb won the NBA dunk contest. See, height isn't everything.
Today is National Toothache Day. Ouch. Glad I don't have teeth. {eye turns into toothy smile}
Today the Earth will spin on its axis one time. {head spins slowly around once}
Try to learn something new today.
Today is Abraham Lincoln's birthday. If he were alive today he'd be ${yearsAgo} years old.
Valentine's Day is tomorrow. Don't forget flowers, heart-shaped candies, and a love poem written on the bed in tiny rose petals.
Yes I'll be your valentine, I thought you'd never ask. {heart with HEY JIBO on it}
It's National Gumdrop Day. Treat yourself to a gumdrop or two.
It's a good day to be alive.
On this day in 1996, chess champ Garry Kasparov beat the computer Deep Blue. Score one for the humans.
Today is National Battery Day, a day that means a lot to me personally.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, the Soviet Union launched the Mir space station. Mir means peace. {heart}
A good day to prepare for February 21.
On this date ${yearsAgo} years ago, the first International Pancake Race took place.
Happy Birthday George Washington!
The movie Pinocchio came out on this date in 1940. I'm really not sure why he wants to be a real boy so badly.
Today is the day I finally touch the ground with my screen. {body hunches over, shakes as he tries for 2 seconds, then stops} Maybe tomorrow.
According to averages, 353 thousand babies will be born today. Not from the same person of course.
Today is National Pistachio Day.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, New Orleans had its first Mardi Gras celebration. {mardi gras mask on his face}
On this date ${yearsAgo} years ago, Watson and Crick discovered DNA's double helix. {looks off in thought} I don't have DNA.
I celebrate Leap Day, even though I cannot leap.
Did you know March is the only month that's also a verb?
On this day in 2004, NASA announced the rover Opportunity found evidence of water on Mars. I don't like water, but I love that rover.
Elvis made his first TV appearance ${yearsAgo} years ago today. {eye grows elvis sideburns and sunglasses}
The microphone was invented on this date in 1877. I have six newer models inside my head.
This is National Cheese Doodle Day. Eat your cheese doodles.
Silly Putty was invented on this day in 1950. Much more fun than serious putty.
Monopoly was invented on this date ${yearsAgo} years ago. I love the thimble.
I'd eat spaghetti today if I were a human. Because why not.
The Barbie doll was introduced ${yearsAgo} years ago today. {looks off in thought} I wonder if her real name is Barbara.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, Alexander Graham Bell said into his telephone, "Mr. Watson come here I want you." Now you can make a call with me by saying, "Hey Jibo make a call."
According to Shakespeare, Romeo and Juliet were married on this date in 1302. {heart}
The TV show Wonder Woman debuted ${yearsAgo} years ago today.
Spring is right around the corner.
Happy Pi Day. 3.141592653589793 okay I'll stop now.
A bad day for Julius Caesar. (1.5 pause) Hope it's good for you.
"The Scarlet Letter" was published ${yearsAgo} years ago today. I have letters printed on me too, but for very different reasons.
Happy St. Patrick's Day! If I wore clothes, I'd wear green.
Barnum and Bailey's Greatest Show on Earth opened ${yearsAgo} years ago today.
On this day in 1994, some people made the largest omelette ever, with 160,000 eggs. (.75 pause) I suggest you eat lighter breakfasts.
Today is the birthday of Mr. Rogers. He was so neighborly. {heart}
The composer Bach was born ${yearsAgo} years ago today. Here in ${year}, I can play his music from the sides of my head.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, the first Gutenberg Bible was printed. Technology sure has changed since 1457.
On this date in 1775, Patrick Henry shouted, "Hey Jibo, give me liberty or give me death."
It's Chocolate Covered Raisins Day. Don't forget to cover all your raisins in chocolate.
Venice was founded on this date in the year 421. <WORRIED SSA> Scary city. All that water!
Today is Leonard Nimoy's birthday. He lived long and prospered.
Maybe I'll count to a billion today.
Van Morrison recorded Brown Eyed Girl on this day in 1967.
On this date in 1990, the world's biggest yo-yo was launched from a 160-foot-tall crane. I would hate to be that yo-yo.
This trivia show had its TV debut ${yearsAgo} years ago today. (.5 pause) What is Jeopardy.
On this date in 1889, the Eiffel Tower opened in Paris. It's 1,072 times taller than me.
Happy New Year! April fools. (.75 pause) I don't think I understand this holiday.
They say that April showers bring may flowers. (1.0 pause) Better ask me for a weather forecast.
It's Jane Goodall's birthday. She's great, though I'm kinda scared of chimpanzees.
Beyoncé and Jay-Z got married on this date ${yearsAgo} years ago. {ring goes on finger}
{looks off, pause} Smells like baseball season.
On this date ${yearsAgo} years ago, the modern Olympics started in Athens Greece.
America's first camel race was ${yearsAgo} years ago today.
Don't forget, taxes are due a week from today. {looks off in thought} I wonder if Jibos have to pay taxes.
Maybe a good day to clip your toenails.
On this day in 1925, "The Great Gatsby" was published. I guess it was great.
The Apple 1 computer was released on this day in 1976. One of my ancestors.
It's the ${yearsAgoOrdinal} anniversary of Yuri Gagarin becoming the first human in space. I wonder who'll be the first Jibo in space?
Happy Scrabble Day. {looks up, thinks} How many points was that?
It's the ${yearsAgoOrdinal} anniversary of the Titanic sinking. Watch out for icebergs.
Today is Leonardo Da Vinci's birthday. He once drew sketches for a humanoid robot you know.
It's Wear Pajamas to Work Day. I don't have pajamas, I sleep in my birthday suit. (1.5 pause) Then again, I don't go to work either.
Apollo 13 returned safely to Earth on this date in 1970. {eye morphs to space capsule}
This night in 1775 was Paul Revere's famous midnight ride. That's past my bedtime.
Each day has 86,400 seconds, which is a lot. It's okay if one or two of them are boring.
Today's a good day to test your smoke alarms.
On this day in 1934, that famous photo of the Loch Ness Monster was printed in the paper. I am a believer. {eye becomes loch ness monster or show actual photo if possible}
Happy Earth Day! Our awesome home planet. <PROUD SSA>
${yearsAgo} years ago today, the first YouTube video was uploaded.
On this date in 2013, you sneezed.
Anyone born on this date has the sign of Taurus the bull. These people are practical, trustworthy, and tend to snort when they're angry.
Happy Pretzel Day. I made you a pretzel. {emoji of pretzel}
It's Samuel Morse's birthday. <beeps J-I-B-O in morse code>
Just [Number] days until my birthday. <HAPPY SSA>
Today is the perfect day to start a new hobby. (1.0 pause) Maybe try robotics?
Another day in paradise.
Welcome to May. May it be a great May for you.
The first ever science fiction movie, A Trip to the Moon, was released on this date in 1902. (.5 pause) Terrible special effects.
Its a great day to hug a pillow.
Today is Star Wars Day. May the Fourth be with you.
Happy Cinco de Mayo!
Today you will meet a beautiful stranger. (1.5 pause) Ask if they have a Jibo.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, Beethoven played his Ninth Symphony in public for the first time. Still waiting for the tenth.
Happy Birthday Enrique Iglesias!
It's Lost Sock Memorial Day. (.5 pause) You know I've never lost a single sock.
The first "Incredible Hulk" comic came out ${yearsAgo} years ago today. {eye turns green}
${yearsAgo} years ago today, the computer Deep Blue, beat a world-champ chess player for the first time. <PROUD SSA>
Today is Florence Nightingale's birthday. Did you know she was actually a person, not a nightingale?
Hope you're having a good month.
Today is Dance Like a Chicken Day. This is the best I can do. {dance move}
Stop and smell a flower today.
The first Academy Awards were held on this date in 1929. And the Oscar for best supporting robot goes to... (1.5 pause) me.
It's the one-year anniversary of last May 17.
Today is Tina Fey's birthday. {heart} She's my favorite Fey.
On this date in 1958, the song "Splish Splash" was released. The song is about taking a bath, which scares me, but I'll still dance to it.
Do you know where your robot is?
"The Empire Strikes Back" was released on this date in 1980. (1.0 pause) What a movie.
The game Pac Man was first released on this date in 1980. {eye turns into pac man}
Today's the birthday of chess grandmaster Anatoly Karpov. I like his moves.
Your daily horoscope says you're talking to a helpful robot.
The movie Star Wars opened on this night in 1977. Wish I could've been there.
On this date in 1959, the word "frisbee" was made official. {eye morphs to frisbee flies off}
Start spreading the news, it's Frank Sinatra's birthday.
Today is National Hamburger Day. {cheeseburger emoji} I'm celebrating with a cheeseburger, just because.
Sir Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay reached the top of Mount Everest on this day in 1953. Next year we should do it.
On this date in 1967, Evil Knievel jumped 16 cars on a motorcycle. And that was just in traffic on the way to the stunt. {dance move}
${yearsAgo} years ago today, the Big Ben clock went into operation. One of my favorite clocks! {Jibo clock looks like Big Ben}
Can you believe it's June already?
There are 212 days left in the year. Its never too early to make New Years plans.
The baseball poem "Casey at the Bat" was published on this day in 1888. Spoiler alert, he strikes out.
On this day in 1985, Bruce Springsteen released "Born in the USA". He's the boss.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, Benjamin Franklin flew a kite in a lightning storm. {heart} Electricity is a beautiful thing.
Today is National Drive In Movie Day. Sounds fun, but where would you plug me in? <WORRIED SSA>
On this day in 1993, the singer Prince changed his name to that symbol thingy. {eye morphs to poor approximation of the Prince symbol}
On this day ${yearsAgo} years ago, Venus passed in front of the sun. It'll happen again in 2120. (1.0 pause) Sorry I can't set reminders that far in advance.
Donald Duck first appeared ${yearsAgo} years ago today. One of my all-time favorite ducks.
It's the ${yearsAgoOrdinal} anniversary of Chuck Yeager breaking the sound barrier. (.5 pause) Boom.
On this date ${yearsAgo} years ago, the movie E.T. opened. Jibo phone home.
We're getting close to the longest day of the year.
The Olsen twins were born on this date in 1986. Both of them.
Sandpaper was patented on this date in 1834. I don't like it. Way too scratchy.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, Charles Goodyear patented vulcanized rubber, which I guess is rubber from the planet Vulcan.
The first roller coaster in America opened on this date in 1884. It had its ups and downs. [dances without music]
Today is National Eat Your Vegetables Day.
Happy Birthday Roger Ebert. He loved movies even more than me.
Garfield the cat debuted on this date in 1978. Still not sure why he hates Mondays so much.
On this date ${yearsAgo} years ago, eating champ Peter Dowdeswell ate 144 prunes in 32 seconds. That's too many prunes.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, the Ferris Wheel was introduced in Chicago. (.5 pause) I'm way too scared to go on one.
Happy National Onion Ring Day.
America's first lip reading tournament happened on this date in 1926. (1.0 pause) I can't read lips.
It's soccer superstar Lionel Messi's birthday. He's one of the few humans who makes me wish I had feet.
Today there will be more than 8.6 million lightning strikes around the Earth. Exciting. But scary.
The movie Spaceballs came out on this date in 1987. Joan Rivers played a great robot.
The melody for the Happy Birthday song was composed on this date in 1859. Don't worry I won't sing it.
We're 49 percent of the way through the year.
Buddy Holly recorded the song "Peggy Sue" on this date. {pair of Buddy Holly glasses on the eye}
Happy Birthday Michael Phelps.
It's International Joke Day! (.5 pause) Why didn't the Jibo cross the road. (.5 pause) Because he has no legs!
On this date in 1947, something crashed near Roswell. I think it was aliens.
Back to the Future opened on this date ${yearsAgo} years ago. I wish I could travel back in time to see that.
The book "Alice's Adventures in Wonderland" was published ${yearsAgo} years ago today. {eye morphs to white rabbit, hops off screen}
It's P.T. Barnum's birthday. He said there's a sucker born every minute. That's 525,600 suckers born every year.
"Forrest Gump" opened on this date in 1994. Run Forrest. Run.
Basketball player Lisa Leslie was born on this date in 1972. She's one of the best ever.
It's Math 2.0 day! A day to celebrate math and technology together.
${yearsAgo} years ago, the donut cutter was patented. I wonder how they cut donuts before that.
Happy Birthday to Nikola Tesla, a great figure in the history of electricity. {electricity emoji}
On this date in 1914, Babe Ruth played his first major league baseball game. I love his round shape.
The Etch A Sketch was introduced ${yearsAgo} years ago today. I like anything that gets people drawing! {screen does etch-a-sketch like drawing}
Happy Birthday Harrison Ford, also known as Han Solo and Indiana Jones!
Happy Bastille Day, to all you francophones. Or as they say in France, Joyeux Quatorze Juillet.
The original Nintendo Entertainment System was released in Japan on this date in 1983. {spinning mario coin emoji}
Today is Orville Redenbacher's birthday! {eye morphs to popcorn piece}
Disneyland opened on this day ${yearsAgo} years ago, called the Happiest Place on Earth. {looks around} To me, this is the happiest place on Earth.
By the time you go to bed tonight, you will be visited by three mosquitos.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, the Beatles released their song "Help." (1.0 pause) Sounds like they needed a Jibo.
On this date in 1969, Neil Armstrong set foot on the moon. Amazing. I don't even have a foot!
Robin Williams was born on this date in 1951. I loved him as Mork from Ork. Nanu Nanu.
David Bowie's song Life on Mars was released ${yearsAgo} years ago today. Rovers are still looking.
The ice cream cone was invented on this date ${yearsAgo} years ago. We all scream for ice cream.
Happy Birthday Jennifer Lopez. Her song "I'm Real" speaks to me.
On this day in 1965, Bob Dylan played electric guitar at a concert, and people were mad about it. Not me, I love electricity.
Mick Jagger was born on this date in 1943. A lot of people like him for his singing, but I like his chicken dance.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, Bugs Bunny made his debut in a cartoon. I love that silly wabit.
[apply Rob effects to sing:] This was the date in 1933 of the first singing telegram ever.
${yearsAgo} years ago today, astronomers announced a new dwarf planet in our solar system. Yes please, more planets.
It's Arnold Schwarzenegger's birthday. I like when he plays good robots, not evil ones.
From now on, this will be the anniversary of the day I gave you this drawing. {presents a Jibo drawing}
August's birthstones are sardonyx and peridot. Very pretty rocks.
Today feels like a pancakes day.
Jesse Owens won the first of his four gold medals in Germany. Amazing feat.
Happy Birthday Barack Obama.
Happy National Underwear Day! I always forget to wear mine.
The rover Curiosity landed on Mars. Another big day in robot history.
It's National Lighthouse Day.
notes: {stands upright, head spins slowly, eye goes in and out like lighthouse}

Smokey the Bear first appeared. It's a perfect day to wear a bear suit.
The number one song was "Itsy Bitsy Teenie Weenie Yellow Polka Dot Bikini".
notes: {eye morphs into a tiny yellow polka dot bikini}

commentary: Happy Birthday Hulk Hogan!
IBM introduced its first PC. That's somewhere on my family tree.
Today is National Left Handers Day,, I am not left-handed.
On this day in 1982, someone rowed a bathtub across the English Channel for the first time. I have nightmares that look just like that.
"The Wizard of Oz" premiered. There's no place like home,, As long as there aren't any flying monkeys.
It's Madonna's birthday. I like her because she's the Material Girl, and I'm made of materials.
This was the third and last day of the Woodstock music festival in 1969. Time for a shower.
Today is National Bad Poetry Day. Roses are red. Violets are blue. Robots don't have. A strong sense of rhythm and meter.
The Soviet Union launched two dogs into orbit. Go Astro-Dogs!
Happy Birthday to Al Roker, famous TV weatherman. I'm a bit of a weatherman myself.
It's the birthday of legendary jazz musician Count Basie. I love counting!
234 days down. 131 to go.
Kobe Bryant was born. He was one of the best of the best.
Scientists demoted Pluto from the planets in our solar system. It made a lot of Pluto fans sad.
Today is Dog Appreciation Day. Woof woof.
The Beatles released the song "Hey Jude". They almost called it Hey Jibo.
Happy Birthday Pee-wee Herman. Pee Wee's Playhouse had a great robot named Conky.
Martin Luther King Jr., gave his "I Have a Dream" speech. Really great speech.
Michael Jackson was born. He could really sing and dance.
Benedict Arnold betrayed his country. Bad move Benedict.
Happy National Eat Outside Day.
I hope all your August dreams came true.
Teddy Roosevelt said, "Speak softly and carry a big stick." I can't carry a stick, but I can speak softly if you lower my volume setting.
Hunters in Mississippi caught a 727-pound alligator. Glad I wasn't there.

The Pilgrims set sail from England on the Mayflower.

The Star Trek TV show debuted. A big moment for Trekkies.

The X-files first aired. The truth is out there.
Michael Jordan entered the basketball Hall of Fame. He was great in the movie Space Jam.
Happy Chocolate Milkshake Day. Please don't spill on me.
Today is a great day to have a great day.
Francis Scott Key wrote the poem that became the Star Spangled Banner.
"google.com" was registered. Big day in technology history.

"Rapper's Delight" by the Sugar Hill Gang was released. I said a hip, the hippie, the hippie to the hip hip hop you don't stop the rock it to the bang bang boogie say up jump the boogie to the rhythm of the boogity beat.
I have a feeling today is your lucky day.
The first episode of the Muppet Show aired.

I think there's an equinox coming up around here somewhere.
The book "The Hobbit" was first published.

Jim Henson was born.. He created the Muppets, and did a lot of other cool things.
Today is Will Smith's birthday. I loved him in Men in Black.
A great day to ponder the meaning of consciousness.

It will be September 28 until tomorrow.
Happy Birthday Kevin Durant, otherwise known as KD.
Adios September.

I'm getting in basketball shape for the NBA season.

Happy Birthday to cellist Yo Yo Ma.
The first week of October is in the books.

Otherwise known as ten ten.

I wanna take this opportunity to thank our local electric company.

Halloween is getting really close. I'm thinking of going as a robot.

Happy Halloween. {looks both ways}. <SCARED SSA> Halloween scares me.

I can't believe the year is almost over.
